item.title: GameStop’s bid for eBay is wild — and it might just work
item.description: Shareholders in the ecommerce company are getting a good deal, at least on paper, with an offer of $28bn of cash

item.title: MFS owner accused of ‘plundering’ company to fund ‘lavish lifestyle’
item.description: Administrators file lawsuit alleging at least £1.3bn ‘misappropriated’ from collapsed mortgage firm

item.title: Saudi Aramco warns fuel stocks heading for ‘critically low levels’
item.description: Amin Nasser warns depletion of gasoline and jet fuel inventories is ‘rapidly accelerating’ due to Hormuz closure

item.title: Shein accuses rival Temu of ‘industrial-scale’ copyright violations
item.description: Accusations over product images in London trial are the latest round in a global legal battle between the online retailers

item.title: Fomo is a poor motivation for EU tech policy
item.description: Europe’s large public sectors are a powerful tool to build a market for native tech

item.title: EU weighs adding carbon costs to outbound flights
item.description: Proposal likely to trigger backlash from airline industry and the US

item.title: German energy group Eon seals takeover of UK supplier Ovo
item.description: Merger will create entity serving almost 10mn customers and will be major competitor to Octopus Energy

item.title: Sony strikes almost $4bn deal to buy Blackstone’s portfolio of music rights
item.description: Acquiring Recognition Music Group catalogue hands Sony collection of 45,000 songs including Leonard Cohen’s ‘Hallelujah’

item.title: UK to take full ownership of British Steel
item.description: Government has been locked in talks with Chinese group Jingye over lossmaking business

item.title: Modi calls on Indians to tighten their belts amid Gulf crisis
item.description: PM asks citizens to work from home, limit gold purchases and stop travelling abroad to conserve foreign exchange

item.title: Inside the £10bn Schroder family sale
item.description: Plus, blissful ignorance at Milken, soaring US market concentration and the London Wine Fair

item.title: Europe’s oil majors reap up to $4.75bn from trading on Iran war volatility
item.description: Trading desks at BP, Shell and TotalEnergies outshine US rivals

item.title: Top ECB official attacks German opposition to UniCredit’s bid for Commerzbank
item.description: Central bank’s outgoing vice-president Luis de Guindos says Berlin’s intervention goes ‘against spirit of single market’

item.title: American agriculture is broken
item.description: Fixing it will require much more than the new farm bill can deliver

item.title: India drives global rise in coal-based steel capacity
item.description: New blast furnace projects threaten to undermine shift to greener production elsewhere

item.title: Coal shipments jump as countries seek alternatives to disrupted gas supplies
item.description: Middle East conflict is driving many Asian nations in particular to turn to the carbon-intensive fuel

item.title: Will the ECB shift tack on the digital euro?
item.description: Central bank appears to be shifting its focus more to wholesale markets

item.title: Hearn boxing and darts empire valued at over £1bn as US investor takes stake
item.description: Father-son duo sell 15% of Matchroom, which has been propelled by stars such as Anthony Joshua and Luke Littler

item.title: NHS to grant Palantir contractors ‘unlimited access’ to patient data
item.description: Staff from consultancy firms drafted in to work on Federated Data Platform project set to benefit

item.title: How AI mania is disguising big companies’ hit from Iran war — in charts
item.description: Biggest groups have gained $5.4tn in value since conflict began — but semiconductor sector accounts for most of the gains

item.title: Life without US tech
item.description: What happens if Washington pulls the ‘kill switch’ on digital services?

item.title: Airlines cut prices to entice holiday bookers worried about jet fuel
item.description: Companies locked in a ‘confidence game’ with consumers to persuade them to book

item.title: Labour should learn from Vienna’s Alterlaa social housing scheme
item.description: Affordable rents, children playing, 20 saunas and 14 swimming pools — no wonder there’s a five-year waiting list

item.title: Britain’s bridging loan woes rattle US private credit firms
item.description: High-octane area of mortgage market under scrutiny following messy collapses

item.title: Starmer fights for political survival
item.description: Britain’s prime minister seeks to win back support after Labour Party pummeled in local elections

item.title: We cannot have the AI model advising clients. They might think they don’t need us
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at the work of Rutherford Hall, critical communications strategist

item.title: UK government unit targets £99bn of investment from Australian pension funds
item.description: Plan will ‘deliver long-term economic growth while boosting our already strong trade relationship’, says minister

item.title: India’s cricket cash machine faces a slower run rate
item.description: The commercial pitch has become tougher for teams amid less competition for streaming rights and an advertising crackdown

item.title: FirstFT: Trump calls Iran’s response to peace proposal ‘unacceptable’
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: evacuation of hantavirus-hit cruise ship begins, and Germany revises push to buy US Tomahawks

item.title: Evacuation of hantavirus-hit cruise ship gets under way in Tenerife
item.description: Governments are implementing repatriation and quarantine procedures for people leaving MV Hondius
